metadata.from: Multiple writers: (1) Dad (James Edward Hanifen Sr.) — page 1 written in pencil, upside down/reversed, signed 'Love Dad'; (2) George and Jan — pages 3-4 written in red ink, signed 'Your buddies George & Jan'
metadata.location_written_from: Des Moines, Iowa (based on return address on envelope); 1220 Fremont St. (George & Jan's address on page 4)
metadata.location_sent_to: P.F.C. John R. Hanifen, 55046284, Co. C 439 Eng. Con. Bn., A.P.O. 301, San Francisco, California
metadata.postmark: Fort Leonard Wood, MO, OCT 31, 11:30 AM, 1951
metadata.envelope_notes: Return address: Nifen Body & Paint Co., 801 Park Street, Des Moines 14, Iowa. 'Bob' written in large handwriting on front of envelope. Two 3-cent Thomas Jefferson stamps. 'Air Mail' notation. '% Postmaster' noted lower left.
metadata.mood_tone: Warm, chatty, and newsy; mixture of casual family updates and mild concern about Bob's business
metadata.legibility_notes: Page 1 is written upside down and must be read rotated 180 degrees; some words partially illegible due to staining and faded ink. Pages 3-4 written in red ink, somewhat faded. Several words uncertain.
metadata.historical_context: John Hanifen is serving in the U.S. Army, addressed as P.F.C. (Private First Class), with an APO address routing through San Francisco, suggesting overseas deployment likely during the Korean War era (1951).

pages.transcription:
Return address: NIFEN BODY & PAINT CO. / 801 Park Street / Des Moines 14, Iowa

[Handwritten annotation on envelope front]: Bob

Addressed to:
P. F. C. John R. Hanifen
55046284
Co. C 439 Eng. Con. Bn.
A. P. O. 301
San Francisco, California

% Postmaster

Postmark: FORT LEONARD WOOD / OCT 31 / 11:30 AM / 1951 / MO.

Two 3-cent United States Postage stamps (Thomas Jefferson). 'Air mail' written in cursive on right side of envelope.

[Note: This page is written upside down relative to the scan. Transcribed after rotating 180 degrees.]

Nov 30-51

Dear John,

Been trying to hear from you — what about
the box. Customs + [illegible] don't you
all do it. [illegible] send some [illegible] the
ell [illegible] from [illegible] letter from you. Have one
looking for [illegible] from Her — well no one
Breaks news from you. I should have gone to
Still Beway. Here I looked at a 51 Ford March
Creston 3a + looks pick up more
bought 4 for you. 4 months [illegible] up more
B [illegible] it up. I, you got [illegible] 9 times.
To us for. Time let me no we got a
Go each time [illegible] Black + white, get boy get
Destination [illegible] Hunting is Tough
if her hear. I heard Hunting is Tough
the of her about 6 all year. 9 am just
Come in. Said said Hello to you. He said
he just don't seem to get Time to Write

We Bought a 48 coupe good deals.
But Need a Body for it. We locked
up 5 Hot last Sat + went Hunting
I got 1 pheasant + 3 Rabbits. Dick Brought
them Back. Kept 1 Rabbit + I haven't
got it yet. We may go Sunday.
Leo is here Now + He is
Writing a Note — So Now I will
Knock it off for You. Bob is not
doing so good — I don't think He should
Haul His Butt, if here He could
at least get By.

Love Dad

[Written in red ink]

Dear John,
      Reed your last letter and
was very glad to hear from you.
Bet you had a time with that M[?]
lewis. I am at your dad's now[?]
asked Jim if old [illegible]
[illegible] he said "I'll [illegible]
going to send him any [illegible]
He hasn't wrote me yet.

      Well we sold our house. had
have to make $2000.00 and took it +
have to [make?] [illegible] apartment house.
I bought me an apartment house.
Have more money to spend now.
      Jan is pregnant, should have
have baby in June. My female
dog is also [pregnant?]
have pups in February.

Mort and Joyce just got back
from California. I guess [his?] is flat
broke again. Pat and Colleen's [illegible]
baby has been in the Hospital
2 months

[Written in red ink, continuation]

the ford is holding up pretty
good, but I thinks I get me a new
Pontiac before long.
      [Saw?] [illegible] Harold & Shirley awhile
back. They are O.K.
      Will be glad to see you when
you get back.
      Write soon —

            Your buddies
            George & Jan.

[Address crossed out]
1220 Fremont St.

full_transcription:
[ENVELOPE]
Return address: NIFEN BODY & PAINT CO. / 801 Park Street / Des Moines 14, Iowa
[Handwritten on envelope]: Bob
Addressed to: P. F. C. John R. Hanifen / 55046284 / Co. C 439 Eng. Con. Bn. / A. P. O. 301 / San Francisco, California
% Postmaster
Postmark: FORT LEONARD WOOD / OCT 31 / 11:30 AM / 1951 / MO.

[PAGE 1 — Dad's letter, written upside down, dated Nov 30-51]

Nov 30-51

Dear John,

Been trying to hear from you — what about the box. Customs + [illegible] don't you all do it. [illegible] send some [illegible] the ell [illegible] from [illegible] letter from you. Have one looking for [illegible] from Her — well no one Breaks news from you. I should have gone to Still Beway. Here I looked at a 51 Ford March Creston 3a + looks pick up more bought 4 for you. 4 months [illegible] up more B [illegible] it up. I, you got [illegible] 9 times. To us for. Time let me no we got a Go each time [illegible] Black + white, get boy get Destination [illegible] Hunting is Tough if her hear. I heard Hunting is Tough the of her about 6 all year. 9 am just Come in. Said said Hello to you. He said he just don't seem to get Time to Write

[PAGE 2 — Dad's letter continued]

We Bought a 48 coupe good deals. But Need a Body for it. We locked up 5 Hot last Sat + went Hunting I got 1 pheasant + 3 Rabbits. Dick Brought them Back. Kept 1 Rabbit + I haven't got it yet. We may go Sunday. Leo is here Now + He is Writing a Note — So Now I will Knock it off for You. Bob is not doing so good — I don't think He should Haul His Butt, if here He could at least get By.

Love Dad

[PAGES 3-4 — George & Jan's letter, written in red ink]

Dear John,
      Reed your last letter and was very glad to hear from you. Bet you had a time with that M[?] lewis. I am at your dad's now[?] asked Jim if old [illegible] [illegible] he said "I'll [illegible] going to send him any [illegible] He hasn't wrote me yet.

      Well we sold our house. had have to make $2000.00 and took it + have to [make?] [illegible] apartment house. I bought me an apartment house. Have more money to spend now.
      Jan is pregnant, should have have baby in June. My female dog is also [pregnant?] have pups in February.

Mort and Joyce just got back from California. I guess [his?] is flat broke again. Pat and Colleen's [illegible] baby has been in the Hospital 2 months

the ford is holding up pretty good, but I thinks I get me a new Pontiac before long.
      [Saw?] [illegible] Harold & Shirley awhile back. They are O.K.
      Will be glad to see you when you get back.
      Write soon —

            Your buddies
            George & Jan.

1220 Fremont St.
summary: This letter to P.F.C. John Hanifen (serving overseas in 1951, likely during the Korean War era) contains two separate notes tucked in the same envelope. The first, from his father (Dad/James Hanifen Sr.), dated November 30, 1951, shares family news including a hunting trip, purchasing a '48 coupe that needs body work, concern about Bob's struggling business, and a visit from Leo. The second note, written in red ink by friends George and Jan, shares personal updates including Jan's pregnancy (baby due in June), the purchase of an apartment house after selling their home, news about mutual friends Mort, Joyce, Pat, Colleen, Harold, and Shirley, and warm wishes for John's safe return.
